subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Near-Earth Asteroids
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Near-Earth Asteroids.

hook.text: Over 34,000 asteroids orbit near Earth. Most are harmless, but a few could devastate entire regions.

buildup.text: NASA tracks them constantly. Any asteroid larger than 140 meters could cause catastrophic damage.

discovery.text: In 2022, NASA's DART mission hit asteroid Dimorphos and changed its orbit. Planetary defense works.

twist.text: We've found most large ones. The real danger is the smaller asteroids we haven't detected yet.

climax.text: The Chelyabinsk meteor in 2013 injured 1,500 in Russia. It was just 20 meters wide—with no warning.

punchline.text: We can deflect them now. Finding them first is the hard part.

quiz.question: What did NASA's DART mission prove?
options: That we can change an asteroid's orbit by deliberately crashing into it
That asteroids are made of loose rubble that falls apart easily
That nuclear weapons are the best way to deflect an asteroid
